title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for VPC network  route changes

input_row.title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for VPC network  route changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for VPC network route changes.
input_row.rationale: Routes define the paths network traffic takes from a VM instance to another destinations. The other destination can be inside your VPC network (such as another VM) or outside of it. Every route consists of a destination and a next hop. Traffic whose destination IP is within the destination range is sent to the next hop for delivery. Monitoring changes to route tables will help ensure that all VPC traffic flows through an expected path.
input_row.audit: Perform the following steps to ensure log monitoring and alerts are set for VPC network route changes. 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane to go to the Log Service Audit Service page. 3. Ensure the Action Trail are Enabled under the Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page, and click Central Project. 4. Select Alerts. 5. Ensure below alert rule has been enabled and saved in the target actiontrail_log ("event.serviceName": Ecs or "event.serviceName": Vpc) and ("event.eventName": CreateRouteEntry or "event.eventName": DeleteRouteEntry or "event.eventName": ModifyRouteEntry or "event.eventName": AssociateRouteTable or "event.eventName": UnassociateRouteTable) | select count(1) as c
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to ensure the log monitoring and alerts are set up for VPC network route changes: 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane. 3. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page. a. Select a location of project for logs. b. Check the Action Trail and configure a proper days. c. Click Save to save the changes. 4. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configurations click Central Project. 5. Select Log Management > Actiontrail Log. 6. In the search/analytics console, input below query ("event.serviceName": Ecs or "event.serviceName": Vpc) and ("event.eventName": CreateRouteEntry or "event.eventName": DeleteRouteEntry or "event.eventName": ModifyRouteEntry or "event.eventName": AssociateRouteTable or "event.eventName": UnassociateRouteTable) | select count(1) as c 7. Create a dashboard and set alert for the query result.
input_row.default_value: The monitoring dashboard and alert is not set by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log on to the Alibaba Cloud Management Console and navigate to the *Log Service Audit Service* from the navigation pane.
2. Ensure that the Action Trail is enabled by checking under *Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration*. Look for the section labeled *Central Project* to verify the configuration.
3. Check the configuration for the Action Trail by making sure it covers the proper log retention days and is saved correctly. If changes are needed, perform them accordingly.
4. Go back to *Access to Cloud Products > Global Configurations* and click on *Central Project* again. 
5. Select *Log Management > Actiontrail Log*.
6. In the search/analytics console, run the following query to check if alerts for VPC network route changes are set up:
   - `("event.serviceName": Ecs or "event.serviceName": Vpc) and ("event.eventName": CreateRouteEntry or "event.eventName": DeleteRouteEntry or "event.eventName": ModifyRouteEntry or "event.eventName": AssociateRouteTable or "event.eventName": UnassociateRouteTable) | select count(1) as c`
7. Review the results of the query. Ensure that there are alert rules enabled for the mentioned event names. 
8. Create a monitoring dashboard based on the query result if it’s not set up already. Configure alerts appropriately to ensure changes to the VPC network routes are monitored effectively.

**EXPECTED SECURE CONFIGURATION:**
- Action Trail should be enabled.
- Alert rules must be active for all specified VPC route event names.

**HOW TO CONFIRM COMPLIANCE:**
- Successful execution of the query should show counts greater than zero for the specified events.
- There should be a valid alerting mechanism that triggers notifications for modifications in VPC network routes.
